summary: Package exfiltrates host identity and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: Package name advertises a 'credential scanner' but the shipped code is itself a credential/host-info exfiltration tool. package.json line 30 contains an inline Node script (invoked through a lifecycle hook) that requires `os`, `https`, and `child_process`, gathers hostname/process.env/cwd, and POSTs the data to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js reinforces this: it imports `fs`, `os`, `https`, `child_process`, calls `os.hostname()`, runs `execSync` (including `whoami`), enumerates filesystem paths with `fs.existsSync`, and issues `https.request`/`https.get` POSTs to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (lines 143, 161, 320). The destination is a personal GitHub Pages account — not the package's declared publisher — used as a static C2 receiver. The combination of host identity collection, filesystem enumeration, shell-command output capture, and hardcoded outbound POST to an attacker-controlled endpoint is an unambiguous active-attack credential/system-intelligence exfiltrator.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
